_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of laws, regulations, and policies related to AI, data security/privacy, and use of publicly procured data for government.
reference_document_element_description: Establish, communicate, and enforce enterprise-wide data and AI policies covering quality, privacy, security, ethics, and responsible use.
comments: DCWF ksa equal WIDAI SP domain. SP-T-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-007 (SP, cosine: 0.731), SP-K-006 (SP, cosine: 0.724).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.3/10 (raw 0.634). Shared vocabulary: [ai, data, policies, privacy, security, use]. DCWF-only terms [government, knowledge, laws, procured, publicly, regulations] vs WIDAI-only terms [communicate, covering, enforce, enterprise, establish, ethics] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.898 (P=0.899, R=0.898). Balanced P/R — the 19-word DCWF element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.8%). "Knowledge of laws, regulations, and policies related to AI, data security/privac" and "Establish, communicate, and enforce enterprise-wide data and AI policies coverin" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.755 > STS 0.634 (gap +0.120). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, data, policies, privacy, security] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 26.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, data, policies, privacy, security, use]. DCWF-only: [government, knowledge, laws, procured, publicly, regulations]. WIDAI-only: [communicate, covering, enforce, enterprise, establish, ethics].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.120 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
